item.title: She Answered One Last Phone Call And Vanished Forever
item.description: Heather Elvis disappeared after receiving calls linked to a married man she had previously been involved with. More than a decade later, nobody has found her, and the mystery continues despite kidnapping convictions connected to the case.

media:content.media:title: She Answered One Last Phone Call And Vanished Forever
media:content.media:description: Heather Elvis disappeared after receiving calls linked to a married man she had previously been involved with. More than a decade later, nobody has found her, and the mystery continues despite kidnapping convictions connected to the case.

item.title: A Deadly Love Triangle: The Murder of Adrianne Jones
item.description:
In 1995, 16 year old Adrianne Jones disappeared from her home in Texas, leaving family members desperate for answers. As investigators searched for clues, suspicion quickly focused on the people closest to her, uncovering a disturbing web of jealousy, betrayal, and manipulation.
Authorities later learned Adrianne had become caught in a volatile relationship dynamic involving her father and his girlfriend. What initially appeared to be a missing person case evolved into a shocking homicide investigation that exposed years of deception and heartbreaking family dysfunction.
The case horrified the nation because of the victim’s age, the people involved, and the devastating circumstances surrounding her death. Decades later, Adrianne Jones' story remains one of the most tragic and disturbing family murder cases in Texas history.

media:content.media:title: A Deadly Love Triangle: The Murder of Adrianne Jones
media:content.media:description:
In 1995, 16 year old Adrianne Jones disappeared from her home in Texas, leaving family members desperate for answers. As investigators searched for clues, suspicion quickly focused on the people closest to her, uncovering a disturbing web of jealousy, betrayal, and manipulation.
Authorities later learned Adrianne had become caught in a volatile relationship dynamic involving her father and his girlfriend. What initially appeared to be a missing person case evolved into a shocking homicide investigation that exposed years of deception and heartbreaking family dysfunction.
The case horrified the nation because of the victim’s age, the people involved, and the devastating circumstances surrounding her death. Decades later, Adrianne Jones' story remains one of the most tragic and disturbing family murder cases in Texas history.

item.title: She Faked Her Own Kidnapping To Hide A Secret
item.description: Chloe Stein sparked a massive search in Pennsylvania after claiming she had been abducted, but investigators later said she made the entire story up to hide the fact that she was no longer enrolled in college and wouldn't be graduating as expected.

media:content.media:title: She Faked Her Own Kidnapping To Hide A Secret
media:content.media:description: Chloe Stein sparked a massive search in Pennsylvania after claiming she had been abducted, but investigators later said she made the entire story up to hide the fact that she was no longer enrolled in college and wouldn't be graduating as expected.

item.title: Woman Convicted of Killing Boyfriend After He Left Her for Miss Ohio
item.description: This video, created by Kendall Rae, delves into the tragic murder of Ryan Poston, a bright young attorney whose life was cut short by his girlfriend, Shayna Hubers.

media:content.media:title: Woman Convicted of Killing Boyfriend After He Left Her for Miss Ohio
media:content.media:description: This video, created by Kendall Rae, delves into the tragic murder of Ryan Poston, a bright young attorney whose life was cut short by his girlfriend, Shayna Hubers.

item.title: Her Best Friends Planned Her Death Weeks In Advance
item.description: The Skylar Neese case horrified the nation after two of her closest friends secretly planned her murder, lured her out late at night, and carried out the attack before pretending to help search for her for months afterward.

media:content.media:title: Her Best Friends Planned Her Death Weeks In Advance
media:content.media:description: The Skylar Neese case horrified the nation after two of her closest friends secretly planned her murder, lured her out late at night, and carried out the attack before pretending to help search for her for months afterward.

item.title: The Great Pyramid Wasn't For The Pharaohs
item.description: The Great Pyramid Wasn't For The Pharaohs

media:content.media:title: The Great Pyramid Wasn't For The Pharaohs
media:content.media:description: The Great Pyramid Wasn't For The Pharaohs

item.title: A Single Drop of Blood Solved a Brutal Attack Nine Years Later
item.description:
In 2008, 17 year old Brittani Marcell was nearly killed after a violent attack inside her Albuquerque, New Mexico home. She suffered devastating injuries and spent years recovering, while investigators struggled to identify the person responsible.
For nearly a decade, the case remained unsolved. The breakthrough came when detectives matched DNA from a single drop of blood left at the scene to Justin Hansen. Investigators later arrested Hansen, and prosecutors argued the DNA evidence conclusively tied him to the attack.
Nine years after the assault, Hansen pleaded no contest to attempted murder and aggravated burglary charges. In 2018, he was sentenced to 18 years in prison, bringing a long awaited measure of justice to Brittani and her family.

media:content.media:title: A Single Drop of Blood Solved a Brutal Attack Nine Years Later
media:content.media:description:
In 2008, 17 year old Brittani Marcell was nearly killed after a violent attack inside her Albuquerque, New Mexico home. She suffered devastating injuries and spent years recovering, while investigators struggled to identify the person responsible.
For nearly a decade, the case remained unsolved. The breakthrough came when detectives matched DNA from a single drop of blood left at the scene to Justin Hansen. Investigators later arrested Hansen, and prosecutors argued the DNA evidence conclusively tied him to the attack.
Nine years after the assault, Hansen pleaded no contest to attempted murder and aggravated burglary charges. In 2018, he was sentenced to 18 years in prison, bringing a long awaited measure of justice to Brittani and her family.

item.title: Jaycee Lee Dugard! A Stolen Life
item.description: This video explores the harrowing abduction, long-term captivity, and miraculous rescue of Jaycee Lee Dugard. It recounts how she was kidnapped at age eleven, held for eighteen years in hidden structures by Phillip and Nancy Garrido, and eventually discovered due to the intuition of observant police officers.

media:content.media:title: Jaycee Lee Dugard! A Stolen Life
media:content.media:description: This video explores the harrowing abduction, long-term captivity, and miraculous rescue of Jaycee Lee Dugard. It recounts how she was kidnapped at age eleven, held for eighteen years in hidden structures by Phillip and Nancy Garrido, and eventually discovered due to the intuition of observant police officers.

item.title: The Tragic End of Heidi Firkus and the Lie That Lasted 13 Years
item.description:
In April 2010, 25 year old Heidi Firkus made a frantic 911 call reporting that someone was trying to break into her Minnesota home. Moments later, a gunshot rang out, and Heidi was found dead from a shotgun wound. Her husband, Nick Firkus, claimed an intruder had broken in and shot them during a struggle.
For years, the case remained unsolved as investigators worked to determine what really happened inside the home that morning. As the investigation continued, authorities uncovered evidence that the couple was facing severe financial problems and were about to lose their home, raising questions about Nick's account of the alleged break in.
More than a decade later, prosecutors charged Nick Firkus with Heidi's murder, arguing that the home invasion story had been fabricated. In 2023, a jury found him guilty of first degree murder, and he was sentenced to life in prison without parole. Heidi's case remains one of Minnesota's most remarkable cold case investigations, ending with answers more than 13 years after her death.

media:content.media:title: The Tragic End of Heidi Firkus and the Lie That Lasted 13 Years
media:content.media:description:
In April 2010, 25 year old Heidi Firkus made a frantic 911 call reporting that someone was trying to break into her Minnesota home. Moments later, a gunshot rang out, and Heidi was found dead from a shotgun wound. Her husband, Nick Firkus, claimed an intruder had broken in and shot them during a struggle.
For years, the case remained unsolved as investigators worked to determine what really happened inside the home that morning. As the investigation continued, authorities uncovered evidence that the couple was facing severe financial problems and were about to lose their home, raising questions about Nick's account of the alleged break in.
More than a decade later, prosecutors charged Nick Firkus with Heidi's murder, arguing that the home invasion story had been fabricated. In 2023, a jury found him guilty of first degree murder, and he was sentenced to life in prison without parole. Heidi's case remains one of Minnesota's most remarkable cold case investigations, ending with answers more than 13 years after her death.

item.title: Brian Walshe Investigation: The Evidence That Built the Case
item.description:
The disappearance of Massachusetts mother Ana Walshe in January 2023 quickly evolved into a major homicide investigation centered on her husband, Brian Walshe. Although Ana's body has never been recovered, prosecutors allege an extensive trail of digital and physical evidence points to what happened after she was last seen.
Investigators uncovered internet searches allegedly made by Brian Walshe that included questions about disposing of a body, cleaning crime scenes, and avoiding detection. Authorities also cited surveillance footage, phone data, DNA evidence, discarded items recovered from trash facilities, and financial records as key parts of their case.
The investigation drew national attention because prosecutors assembled a largely circumstantial case without recovering the victim's remains. Brian Walshe has pleaded not guilty, and the case continues to be closely followed as one of the most high profile missing person investigations in recent years.

media:content.media:title: Brian Walshe Investigation: The Evidence That Built the Case
media:content.media:description:
The disappearance of Massachusetts mother Ana Walshe in January 2023 quickly evolved into a major homicide investigation centered on her husband, Brian Walshe. Although Ana's body has never been recovered, prosecutors allege an extensive trail of digital and physical evidence points to what happened after she was last seen.
Investigators uncovered internet searches allegedly made by Brian Walshe that included questions about disposing of a body, cleaning crime scenes, and avoiding detection. Authorities also cited surveillance footage, phone data, DNA evidence, discarded items recovered from trash facilities, and financial records as key parts of their case.
The investigation drew national attention because prosecutors assembled a largely circumstantial case without recovering the victim's remains. Brian Walshe has pleaded not guilty, and the case continues to be closely followed as one of the most high profile missing person investigations in recent years.

item.title: Who Killed Elizabeth Barraza? The Mystery That Still Has No Answers
item.description:
In January 2019, 29 year old Elizabeth "Liz" Barraza was setting up a garage sale outside her Texas home when a mysterious figure approached her driveway and opened fire. The attack was captured on surveillance video, yet more than seven years later, no one has been arrested.
Investigators believe the killing was targeted, pointing to the suspect's timing, behavior, and apparent knowledge of Liz's plans that morning. Despite thousands of tips, surveillance footage, and years of investigation, the identity of the killer remains unknown.
The case continues to frustrate investigators and devastate Liz's family, who are still searching for answers in one of Texas' most baffling unsolved murder mysteries.

media:content.media:title: Who Killed Elizabeth Barraza? The Mystery That Still Has No Answers
media:content.media:description:
In January 2019, 29 year old Elizabeth "Liz" Barraza was setting up a garage sale outside her Texas home when a mysterious figure approached her driveway and opened fire. The attack was captured on surveillance video, yet more than seven years later, no one has been arrested.
Investigators believe the killing was targeted, pointing to the suspect's timing, behavior, and apparent knowledge of Liz's plans that morning. Despite thousands of tips, surveillance footage, and years of investigation, the identity of the killer remains unknown.
The case continues to frustrate investigators and devastate Liz's family, who are still searching for answers in one of Texas' most baffling unsolved murder mysteries.

item.title: Her Husband Plotted to End Her Life With Poison But She Survived!
item.description: This video by Kendall Rae details the true crime survival story of Hannah Pettey, who was systematically poisoned with lead by her husband, Brian Man, a chiropractor from Decatur, Alabama. Under the guise of health supplements, Brian slowly poisoned Hannah while isolating her and creating a financial motive through secret life insurance policies. Following her near-fatal decline and eventual medical diagnosis, a subsequent investigation and trial led to Brian's conviction for attempted murder in 2025. The case highlights both the chilling reality of this domestic betrayal and Hannah's remarkable resilience in her recovery.

media:content.media:title: Her Husband Plotted to End Her Life With Poison But She Survived!
media:content.media:description: This video by Kendall Rae details the true crime survival story of Hannah Pettey, who was systematically poisoned with lead by her husband, Brian Man, a chiropractor from Decatur, Alabama. Under the guise of health supplements, Brian slowly poisoned Hannah while isolating her and creating a financial motive through secret life insurance policies. Following her near-fatal decline and eventual medical diagnosis, a subsequent investigation and trial led to Brian's conviction for attempted murder in 2025. The case highlights both the chilling reality of this domestic betrayal and Hannah's remarkable resilience in her recovery.

item.title: Why Did Ibraheem Yazeed Murder Aniah Blanchard?
item.description:
In October 2019, 19 year old Aniah Blanchard disappeared after stopping at a gas station in Auburn, Alabama. Investigators later determined she had been abducted and fatally shot. Her body was discovered weeks later in a wooded area, leading authorities to charge Ibraheem Yazeed with her murder.
One of the biggest questions in the case has always been motive. Prosecutors established that Yazeed kidnapped and killed Blanchard, but no clear personal connection between the two was ever uncovered. Evidence presented by investigators suggested the encounter was random rather than targeted. Surveillance footage showed the two at the same gas station, and a witness reported seeing Yazeed force Aniah into her vehicle.
Authorities have never publicly provided a definitive explanation for why Yazeed chose Aniah or what his exact motive was. As a result, the reason behind the crime remains one of the most disturbing unanswered aspects of the case. In 2026, Yazeed was convicted of murder and felony murder and later sentenced to life in prison without parole.

media:content.media:title: Why Did Ibraheem Yazeed Murder Aniah Blanchard?
media:content.media:description:
In October 2019, 19 year old Aniah Blanchard disappeared after stopping at a gas station in Auburn, Alabama. Investigators later determined she had been abducted and fatally shot. Her body was discovered weeks later in a wooded area, leading authorities to charge Ibraheem Yazeed with her murder.
One of the biggest questions in the case has always been motive. Prosecutors established that Yazeed kidnapped and killed Blanchard, but no clear personal connection between the two was ever uncovered. Evidence presented by investigators suggested the encounter was random rather than targeted. Surveillance footage showed the two at the same gas station, and a witness reported seeing Yazeed force Aniah into her vehicle.
Authorities have never publicly provided a definitive explanation for why Yazeed chose Aniah or what his exact motive was. As a result, the reason behind the crime remains one of the most disturbing unanswered aspects of the case. In 2026, Yazeed was convicted of murder and felony murder and later sentenced to life in prison without parole.

item.title: Her Poison Expert Husband Allegedly Did What?!
item.description: This video covers the tragic case of Betty Bowman, a pharmacist at the Mayo Clinic who was poisoned and killed by her husband, Connor Bowman.

media:content.media:title: Her Poison Expert Husband Allegedly Did What?!
media:content.media:description: This video covers the tragic case of Betty Bowman, a pharmacist at the Mayo Clinic who was poisoned and killed by her husband, Connor Bowman.

item.title: She Walked Into the Desert at Dawn and Was Never Seen Again
item.description:
In October 2018, 16 year old Karlie Gusé vanished from her family’s home in Chalfant, California after a frightening night in which loved ones said she appeared confused, fearful, and disoriented. By sunrise, Karlie had left the house without her phone, money, or personal belongings and was seen walking alone near Highway 6.
Multiple witnesses reported seeing Karlie that morning, but despite extensive searches involving helicopters, dogs, and law enforcement agencies, no confirmed trace of her has ever been found. Investigators have explored numerous possibilities over the years, yet no theory has fully explained how she seemingly vanished without a clue.
More than seven years later, Karlie's disappearance remains one of California's most haunting unsolved mysteries. The FBI and Mono County Sheriff's Office continue to seek information about what happened during the final hours before she disappeared.

media:content.media:title: She Walked Into the Desert at Dawn and Was Never Seen Again
media:content.media:description:
In October 2018, 16 year old Karlie Gusé vanished from her family’s home in Chalfant, California after a frightening night in which loved ones said she appeared confused, fearful, and disoriented. By sunrise, Karlie had left the house without her phone, money, or personal belongings and was seen walking alone near Highway 6.
Multiple witnesses reported seeing Karlie that morning, but despite extensive searches involving helicopters, dogs, and law enforcement agencies, no confirmed trace of her has ever been found. Investigators have explored numerous possibilities over the years, yet no theory has fully explained how she seemingly vanished without a clue.
More than seven years later, Karlie's disappearance remains one of California's most haunting unsolved mysteries. The FBI and Mono County Sheriff's Office continue to seek information about what happened during the final hours before she disappeared.

item.title: The Only Unsolved Skyjacking In American History
item.description:
On November 24, 1971, a man using the name D.B. Cooper boarded a commercial flight, claimed he had a bomb, and demanded $200,000 in ransom before parachuting from the aircraft somewhere over the Pacific Northwest. Despite one of the largest manhunts in FBI history, Cooper was never found.
Investigators spent decades pursuing leads, interviewing suspects, and analyzing physical evidence, but no theory has ever definitively explained who Cooper was or whether he survived the jump. In 1980, a portion of the ransom money was discovered along the Columbia River, only deepening the mystery.
The case remains the only unsolved skyjacking in American history and continues to fascinate investigators, journalists, and true crime enthusiasts more than 50 years later. To this day, the true identity and fate of D.B. Cooper remain unknown.

media:content.media:title: The Only Unsolved Skyjacking In American History
media:content.media:description:
On November 24, 1971, a man using the name D.B. Cooper boarded a commercial flight, claimed he had a bomb, and demanded $200,000 in ransom before parachuting from the aircraft somewhere over the Pacific Northwest. Despite one of the largest manhunts in FBI history, Cooper was never found.
Investigators spent decades pursuing leads, interviewing suspects, and analyzing physical evidence, but no theory has ever definitively explained who Cooper was or whether he survived the jump. In 1980, a portion of the ransom money was discovered along the Columbia River, only deepening the mystery.
The case remains the only unsolved skyjacking in American history and continues to fascinate investigators, journalists, and true crime enthusiasts more than 50 years later. To this day, the true identity and fate of D.B. Cooper remain unknown.

item.title: The Incredible Survival Of Nicki Lenway
item.description:
In 2022, Minneapolis crime scene investigator Nicki Lenway was ambushed while picking up her son from a parenting center. A masked attacker ran up behind her and shot her at point blank range before leaving her for dead in a parking lot. Despite suffering devastating injuries, Nicki survived the attack.
Investigators quickly discovered the shooting was far from random. Surveillance footage, digital evidence, and phone records helped uncover a murder for hire plot tied to a bitter custody dispute involving Nicki’s ex boyfriend, Tim Amacher, and his girlfriend, Colleen Larson.
The case gained national attention because of Nicki’s remarkable survival, the extensive digital trail that exposed the conspiracy, and the shocking lengths the perpetrators allegedly went to in an effort to silence her forever.

media:content.media:title: The Incredible Survival Of Nicki Lenway
media:content.media:description:
In 2022, Minneapolis crime scene investigator Nicki Lenway was ambushed while picking up her son from a parenting center. A masked attacker ran up behind her and shot her at point blank range before leaving her for dead in a parking lot. Despite suffering devastating injuries, Nicki survived the attack.
Investigators quickly discovered the shooting was far from random. Surveillance footage, digital evidence, and phone records helped uncover a murder for hire plot tied to a bitter custody dispute involving Nicki’s ex boyfriend, Tim Amacher, and his girlfriend, Colleen Larson.
The case gained national attention because of Nicki’s remarkable survival, the extensive digital trail that exposed the conspiracy, and the shocking lengths the perpetrators allegedly went to in an effort to silence her forever.

item.title: The Disappearance of Leeanna Warner
item.description:
In 2003, 5 year old Leeanna Warner vanished while walking home from a friend’s house in Chisholm, Minnesota. The short walk should have taken only minutes, but Leeanna never made it back, triggering an immediate search across the small community.
Investigators spent years following leads, interviewing potential suspects, and examining possible sightings, but no confirmed trace of Leeanna has ever been found. Authorities have long believed foul play was involved, though the case remains officially unsolved.
Leeanna’s disappearance continues to haunt Minnesota decades later, with her family and investigators still searching for answers about what happened during the brief walk home that changed everything.

media:content.media:title: The Disappearance of Leeanna Warner
media:content.media:description:
In 2003, 5 year old Leeanna Warner vanished while walking home from a friend’s house in Chisholm, Minnesota. The short walk should have taken only minutes, but Leeanna never made it back, triggering an immediate search across the small community.
Investigators spent years following leads, interviewing potential suspects, and examining possible sightings, but no confirmed trace of Leeanna has ever been found. Authorities have long believed foul play was involved, though the case remains officially unsolved.
Leeanna’s disappearance continues to haunt Minnesota decades later, with her family and investigators still searching for answers about what happened during the brief walk home that changed everything.
